reproduction_targets.target: Exact string search with Boyer-Moore right-to-left comparisons and bad-character plus good-suffix shifts
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: Matches Python reference substring search on fixed examples and 100 randomized smoke cases.

reproduction_targets.target: Synthetic analogue of the paper's Section 6 empirical trend
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: For deterministic substitute sources, length-5 English-like and 100-character-alphabet average references per character passed are below the KMP reference rate of 1.0, and those two curves generally decrease as pattern length increases.

datasets.name: Synthetic Section 6 substitute sources
datasets.substitute_strategy: Use binary random, extracted-paper English text, and 100-character random sources because the paper's original source strings are unavailable.

metrics.name: references per character passed
metrics.reported_value: English length-5 example reported as approximately 0.24 in the paper

baselines.name: Naive exact substring search
baselines.reported_result: Used as an implementation-local comparison-count baseline

experiments.name: Section 6 synthetic analogue
experiments.purpose: Compare average references per character passed across source types and pattern lengths.

assumptions: Use zero-based Python offsets while documenting that the paper presents one-based pseudocode.
Count each text-pattern character comparison as one string reference.
Use deterministic substitute corpora for the unavailable original random/manual corpora.
Evaluate trend reproduction rather than exact PDP-10 instruction-count values.

missing_but_required.item: Original benchmark corpora and random samples
missing_but_required.impact: Exact Figure 1 numeric curve cannot be reproduced.

missing_but_required.item: PDP-10 assembly implementation and instruction accounting
missing_but_required.impact: Figure 2 machine-instruction results cannot be reproduced.
